metadata.title: Letter to Mr. Murray Sheehan regarding commencement, 1942-05-26
metadata.creator: anonymous
metadata.description: A letter addressed to Mr. Murray Sheehan from the President regarding plans for commencement at a university, dated May 26, 1942.
metadata.transcript:
May 26, 1942

Mr. Murray Sheehan  
Royal Thai Legation  
2300 Kalorama Road  
Washington, D. C.

Dear Murray:

I am delighted to know that you are coming on for  
Commencement. We shall all be glad to see you and I think  
you will find yourself very comfortable in David Swing Hall  
where we are making a reservation for you.

It is too bad that we cannot have at least one boy  
from Thailand in the graduating class while you are here,  
particularly since we were unable to secure the Minister  
as our speaker.

Cordially yours,

President

It is too bad that we cannot have at least one boy from Thailand in the graduating class while you are here, particularly since we were unable to secure the Minister as our speaker. Cordially yours, President
